message:
fix(wills-share): title and subtitle now match article, no attorney framing

The wills-and-estate-planning share cards on X, iMessage, Telegram, and
every other crawler were leading with "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on
a Saturday." — a tagline, not the actual article title — and the
subtitle and tag pill both pushed an "attorney signs the docs" framing
that misrepresents the piece. The article is an AI skill for drafting
the intake; nothing in it requires an attorney, and the share image
shouldn't imply it does.

Copy changes (dynamic opengraph-image.tsx + twitter-image.tsx + the
OG_IMAGE_ALT const in page.tsx):

  pill:     "Life Planning · AI Agents · Attorney Review"
            -> "Wills · Estates · AI Agents"

  H1 ln 1:  "Drafting a Serious"
            -> "An AI Skill for"
  H1 ln 2:  "Estate Plan on a Saturday."
            -> "Wills & Estate Planning"
            (together: the exact article title)

  subtitle: "AI agents · paid skill · attorney signs the docs."
            -> "A Claude Code + Codex skill for estate-planning intake."

  alt:      "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday | …"
            -> "An AI Skill for Wills & Estate Planning | …"

Stat chips (201 files / 17 subagents / 45 templates) are untouched —
they're facts from the article, not copy.

Pass checked against /de-slopify: no emdashes, no "Here's why",
no "It's not X, it's Y", no forced enthusiasm, no hedges, no pseudo-
profound openers. Middle-dot separators (·) are kept as the visual
rhythm of the pill and subtitle, consistent with the existing card.

public/og/wills-and-estate-planning-{opengraph,twitter}.jpg regenerated
off the new dynamic routes via the same sharp(quality=85, progressive,
mozjpeg) pipeline the prerender:og script uses. 93KB / 88KB.
